assertions.expectation: Explains that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict but several tools help narrow the window
assertions.reason: Response acknowledges that exact dates can't be predicted but provides multiple tools and mechanisms to narrow timing windows and increase predictability

assertions.expectation: Mentions the GKE release schedule as a 'best case' timeline — upgrades won't happen before listed dates
assertions.reason: Response mentions the GKE Release Schedule and explains it shows when new versions become available and provides estimated auto-upgrade dates with advance notice

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status for the current target version and any pauses
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command 'gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info' and explains it shows autoUpgradeStatus, target versions, and timeline information

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new releases roll out progressively across regions over 4-5 business days, and the version promotion path includes channel promotion (Rapid → Regular → Stable)
assertions.reason: Response explains channel timing characteristics showing progression from Rapid to Regular to Stable with specific timing differences (2-6 weeks between channels)

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows so upgrades only happen during known time slots. May note that for ultimate predictability, customers can initiate upgrades themselves instead of waiting for auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed maintenance window configuration commands and explicitly mentions user-initiated upgrades for maximum control as an alternative to auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance, opt-in via Cloud Logging) or the get-upgrade-info API for checking target versions programmatically
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions 72-hour advance notifications via Cloud Logging, provides the command to enable them, and shows the get-upgrade-info API command

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE upgrade visibility shows the cluster's target version and EoL timeline
assertions.reason: Response explains that get-upgrade-info shows target versions and endOfStandardSupportTimestamp, providing visibility into upgrade targets and EoL timelines

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing (advanced feature, limited adoption) as an option for multi-cluster predictability — not a primary recommendation
assertions.reason: Response includes a section on 'Multi-cluster Progressive Rollout' with fleet clusterupgrade commands, treating it as an additional option for large fleets rather than a primary recommendation
